attack_summary: An ICMP flood originating from a compromised Ubuntu host (hping3) targeting the enterprise LAN and Internet path, causing potential resource exhaustion and service disruption.

mitigations.name: Remove/disable hping3 on compromised host
mitigations.brief_description: Disabled/removed the hping3 binary on the compromised Ubuntu host to eliminate local ICMP flood capability.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

mitigations.name: Enable control-plane protection and egress blocking (CoPP and FortiGate rate-limiting)
mitigations.brief_description: Configured Cisco CoPP and FortiGate policies intended to block ICMP egress and protect device CPU, but the ICMP flood continued due to a rule/scope mismatch or logical gap.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

mitigations.name: QoS / traffic shaping (ingress policing + low-priority queue)
mitigations.brief_description: Applied token-bucket policing and low-priority queuing on FortiGate and access switch to deprioritize and shape ICMP traffic, preventing saturation while preserving legitimate management and HTTP traffic.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

key_observations: Host-level removal of the attack tool immediately stopped local attack traffic but does not remove persistence or prevent re-installation.
Network egress/control-plane rules can fail if selectors (source IP/interface/zone) or rule scope are incorrect; validation showed the CoPP/egress attempt did not interrupt the flood.
QoS/traffic-shaping successfully preserved availability by rate-limiting volumetric ICMP traffic without breaking legitimate LAN pings or external HTTP connectivity.
Successful mitigations preserved required connectivity (LAN ping and external HTTP checks) while reducing attack impact.
Defenses are complementary: host hardening plus properly scoped network controls and QoS provide layered protection.
overall_conclusion: A layered approach was effective: disabling the offending tool on the host and applying QoS/ingress policing both stopped the ICMP flood while preserving legitimate traffic. The attempted control-plane/egress policy failed due to configuration scope issues and should be reviewed and corrected. Follow-up actions should include removing persistence on the host, hardening host and network controls, validating rule scopes, and retaining QoS to protect availability against future volumetric floods.
